item.title: Uruguay Central Bank Chief Sees Better Growth in First Quarter
item.description: Uruguay’s economy should bounce back from weak growth in the second half of last year to expand by just under 1% in this year’s first quarter, according to the country’s top central banker.

item.title: Tory Burch Sweetens Terms on Loan to Buy Out General Atlantic
item.description: Luxury retailer Tory Burch LLC sweetened terms on a $700 million leveraged loan intended to help it repurchase private equity firm General Atlantic’s stake.

item.title: Fertilizer Prices Drop as Hormuz Opening Brings Farmers Relief
item.description: Fertilizer prices were dropping sharply Friday after Iran said the Strait of Hormuz was open, bringing relief to farmers even as they still face delays in deliveries.

item.title: Allbirds’ AI Pivot Found A Buyer in the Rebirthed MEME Fund
item.description: A rebirthed meme fund was ready to pounce on Allbird’s AI pivot

item.title: Kazaks Warns Against Assuming ECB’s Next Move Will Be a Hike
item.description: It’s not a given that the European Central Bank’s next move on interest rates will be a hike, according to Governing Council member Martins Kazaks.

item.title: IMF Warns Suspending EU Carbon Market Comes With Huge Risks
item.description: The International Monetary Fund urged the European Union not to pause its emissions trading system, warning it would come with “huge” risks that might jeopardize investment.

item.title: ECB’s Kocher Warns Against Preemptive Rate Action on Uncertainty
item.description: The European Central Bank must avoid knee-jerk policy action over the conflict in the Middle East, according to Governing Council member Martin Kocher.

item.title: Codelco Targets Higher 2027 Output to Reclaim Top Copper Spot
item.description: Codelco is targeting a slight increase in copper output in 2027 as Chile’s state-owned miner looks to reclaim the mantle of world’s biggest supplier of the industrial metal.

item.title: An Unqualified Good Year For Stocks
item.description: Are we headed back to US underperformance?

item.title: Macklem Warns Against Hiking Too Early or Too Late on Oil Shock
item.description: Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem said central banks around the world are positioned differently when it comes to economic slack and inflation, and their responses to the oil price shock are likely to vary.

item.title: Oil Tankers Hauling US Crude Via Panama Approaching 4-Year High
item.description: US oil cargoes transiting the Panama Canal are close to a four-year high as Asian refiners rush to import American crude in lieu of Mideast supplies strangled by the weeks-long disruption to Strait of Hormuz shipping.

item.title: Retail Traders Make Korea Top Place for Risky Exchange Products
item.description: South Korean investors’ appetite for risk has turned the Korea Exchange into the world’s busiest hub for risky leveraged vehicles.

item.title: Dollar Shortage Still Looms After Argentina Buys Time at IMF
item.description: Argentina bought itself time at the International Monetary Fund this week, but President Javier Milei still faces a hard-currency shortage that he’ll have to tackle before elections next year.

item.title: Disney Debuts Giant Screen Brand for Cinemas to Rival Imax
item.description: Walt Disney Co. is partnering with movie theater chains on a new large-format screen certification that will roll out in time for the release of Avengers: Doomsday in December.

item.title: AI Coding Startup Cursor In Talks to Raise $2 Billion in Funding
item.description: Cursor, a leading artificial intelligence startup for coding, is in advanced talks with investors to raise about $2 billion in a funding round at a valuation of more than $50 billion, not including the investment, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Short Squeeze Turbocharges Equity Rally as Riskiest Stocks Soar
item.description: Investors could point to any number of reasons behind the stock market’s neck-breaking rally. A rapid unwind in bearish wagers gone wrong should be near the top of the list.

item.title: Palantir, Thales Among Companies Competing on New FAA AI Tool
item.description: The US Federal Aviation Administration has brought in Palantir Technologies Inc., Thales SA and Air Space Intelligence Inc. to compete on a new artificial intelligence tool for air traffic management, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Ethiopia Bondholders Start Process to Sue Government Over Defaulted Debt
item.description: A group of Ethiopia’s bondholders formally began a process to sue the government over a defaulted $1 billion debt, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Court Studies Decree That Led to Colombian Central Bank Crisis
item.description: A Colombian court agreed to study a three-decade-old decree that threatens the central bank’s ability to raise interest rates.

item.title: SNB’s Schlegel Stresses Higher Willingness on FX Interventions
item.description: The Swiss National Bank is more willing than usual to step into markets to ensure price stability amid higher energy prices, according to President Martin Schlegel.

item.title: The Imagine Dragons Brothers Try to Take on the Video-Game Market
item.description: With ‘Last Flag,’ lifelong gamers Dan and Mac Reynolds are trying to enter an overcrowded market

item.title: Fed’s Waller Signals Caution On Rate Cuts, Sees Risk of Longer Conflict
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said he is cautious about the need to lower interest rates in the near term, due to the energy shock triggered by war in Iran, and warned of the risk of a prolonged impact on inflation due to the conflict.

item.title: Brazil Treasury Sees Room for More Foreign-Exchange-Linked Debt
item.description: Brazil’s government sees room to grow its share of foreign-exchange-linked debt even further following this year’s planned expansion, Treasury Secretary Daniel Leal said in an interview.

item.title: Anthropic’s Amodei to Meet Wiles With US Seeking Mythos Access
item.description: Anthropic PBC Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei is scheduled to meet on Friday with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, according to people familiar with the matter, as the Trump administration seeks wider US government access to the company’s powerful new Mythos artificial intelligence model.

item.title: Obesity Firm Kailera Therapeutics Up 63% After $625 Million IPO
item.description: Kailera Therapeutics Inc. shares surged 63% after the clinical-stage biotechnology company focused on obesity raised $625 million in an upsized US initial public offering.

item.title: Oil Tankers Make Dash Toward Hormuz as Iran Says Strait Is Open
item.description: At least eight oil tankers raced toward the Strait of Hormuz in the hours after Iran’s foreign minister said the vital waterway was fully open to shipping.

item.title: Hedge Funds’ Treasury Bets Risk Market Shock, Apollo’s Slok Warns
item.description: A buildup of leveraged hedge fund bets in Treasuries has left investors exposed to abrupt position shifts that could amplify stress across global bond markets, according to Apollo Global Management Chief Economist Torsten Slok.

item.title: Short Seller Targets AQR Backer Over Tax-Loss Harvesting
item.description: A short seller is taking aim at the more than $1 trillion that’s invested in strategies that reduce taxes for the rich — putting one of the US’ most staid money managers in its crosshairs.

item.title: Costa Rica Says China Failed to Fulfill Trade Deal Expectations
item.description: Costa Rica is dissatisfied with the volume of its exports to China under its free trade deal with the Asian giant, according to a minister from the Central American nation.

item.title: Alcoa Is Close to a Deal to Sell Smelter Site to Bitcoin Miner
item.description: Alcoa Corp. is in advanced talks to sell a former smelter site in upstate New York to Bitcoin mining firm NYDIG as part of its plans to dispose of its dormant assets.
